input.paid_tier: $9/month — Friday 'The Sources' piece (1,200 words, primary documents) + quarterly reading list
input.testimonial: The Friday piece is what I look forward to most. Used it in three policy briefings. — Policy Analyst

test_cases.output_summary: 240-word pitch. Opens with gracious acknowledgment of free reader relationship, not guilt. 'The Sources' benefit described specifically (1,200 words, primary documents, what was cut). Price stated explicitly ($9/$90). Single paid subscriber quote. One CTA button. Closes with reassurance that free essay continues. Calm authoritative register throughout.

input.paid_tier: $12/month — Monthly live data teardown session (60 min, recorded) + access to a running database of UX research findings with source links
input.offer: Launch discount: $8/month for first 3 months for subscribers who join before end of month

test_cases.output_summary: 220-word pitch in sharp sardonic voice. Live teardown described concretely (60 min, what happens in them). Database benefit is specific (running, sourced). Launch discount presented plainly with clear deadline — no false-scarcity language. Price comparison is factual not coffee-metaphor. Three CTA button options. Voice consistent throughout.

input.paid_tier: $7/month — Monthly supplier directory update (verified wholesale contacts, negotiated pricing info from reader network) + seasonal planning templates
input.testimonial: The supplier directory alone has saved me more than the subscription costs in better wholesale prices. — market gardener, 2 acres

test_cases.output_summary: 180-word pitch in unpretentious practical voice. Supplier directory benefit is concrete (verified contacts, pricing info). Seasonal templates described briefly but specifically. $7/month price stated plainly. Testimonial from a market gardener feels authentic and specific to the audience. CTA options practical not salesy.
